original: Roman Emperor Pertinax is assassinated by his Praetorian Guards, who then sell the throne in an auction to Didius Julianus
Roman Emperor Valentinian I appoints his brother Flavius Valens co-emperor
Pope Hormisdas reunites the Eastern and Western church, ending the Acacian schism in a ceremony in the cathedral of Constantinople
Bloemkamp Abbey (Oldeklooster) attacked & destroyed in Friesland
Origin of the Fasli Era in India
Foundation stone laid for the new city of La Valletta in Malta by Knights Hospitaller Grand Master Jean de Valette
Parliament sends an address to the king requesting that he demand redress from Spain (War of Jenkin's Ear)
Britain passes Coercive Act against Massachusetts
Juan Bautista de Anza finds the site for the Presidio of San Francisco
Allies under the prince of Coburg defeat French forces at Le Cateau
Louvre opens to the public (although officially opened since August)
The Duchy of Courland, a northern fief of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, ceases to exist and becomes part of Imperial Russia during the partitions of Poland
Bethel African Methodist Church of Philadelphia is 1st US-African church
Nathaniel Briggs of New Hampshire patents a washing machine
Wilhelm Olbers discovers 2 Pallas, the second known asteroid
Ohio passes law restricting movement of Blacks

Battle of Medellín, Peninsular War: French forces under Marshal Claude Victor defeat the Spanish under General Gregorio Garcia de la Cuesta, killing or wounding 8,000 Spanish soldiers and opening the way to Southern Spain
Javanese Prince Diponegoro, leader of resistance against Dutch colonial rule is arrested by Dutch authorities during peace negotiations
Jose Zorilla's "Don Juan Tenorio" premieres in Madrid
Mexico drops diplomatic relations with US
Great Britain and France declare war on Russia, expanding the Crimean War
First Taranaki War: Battle of Waireka begins in New Zealand between Europeans and Māori
Skirmish at Bealeton Station, Virginia
First ambulance goes into service
San Francisco Art Association holds open reception at 430 Pine
1st World Weightlifting Championship is won by Edward Lawrence in London
Umberto Giordano's opera "Andrea Chenier" premieres at La Scala Teatro in Milan, Italy, with Giuseppe Borgatti singing the title role, and libretto by Luigi Illica
27.9 cm precipitation at McMinnville, Tennessee (state record)
The Lawa railway from Paramaribo to Dam railway opens in Suriname
Guatemala becomes a signatory to the Buenos Aires copyright treaty
Jews are expelled from Tel Aviv & Jaffa by Turkish authorities
Puccini's "La Rondine" premieres in Monte Carlo
Anton Denkin's White Russian troops defeated by Bolsheviks and Soviets at Novorossijsk on the Black Sea (The British aid Denkin in his escape)
Palm Sunday tornado outbreak of 1920 affects the Great Lakes region and Deep South states
1st microfilm device introduced
Stanley Cup Final, Mutual Street Arena, Toronto, ON: Toronto St Patricks (NHL) beat Vancouver Millionaires (PCHA), 5-1 for a 3-2 series win
WGN-AM in Chicago IL begins radio transmissions

Majestic Theater opens at 245 W 44th St, NYC; designed with a modern Spanish exterior and French revival style interior by architect Herbert J. Krapp, it is largest of 3 theater and hotel development cuilt by Irving Chanin
Democratic constitution goes into effect in Ecuador
Construction begins of the exhibition center to host the Thessaloniki International Trade Fair
WWII: Two Dutch Fokker fighter plane crews shoot down British RAF bomber near Rotterdam
Sea battle at Cape Matapan: British fleet under Cunningham defeats Italy
-29] 234 RAF bombers attack Lubeck

St. Nazaire Raid (Operation Chariot): obsolete British destroyer HMS Campbeltown, rigged with explosives and flying German flags, rams gates of German occupied St Nazaire port in France, and self detonates; kills 360 Germans, and disables the dry dock for duration of the war
Last German V-1 (buzz bomb) attack on London
US State Department releases the Acheson-Lilienthal Report, outlining a plan for the international control of nuclear power
English astronomer Fred Hoyle coins the term "Big Bang" in a BBC radio talk while actually arguing for the alternative "steady-state" theory
"Stock exchanges open, dikes closed" raises 5,200,000 guilders
7th Tony Awards: "The Crucible" (Outstanding Play) & "Wonderful Town" (Outstanding Musical) win
KCAU TV channel 9 in Sioux City, IA (ABC) begins broadcasting
8th Tony Awards: "Teahouse of the August Moon" (Best Play) & "Kismet" (Best Musical) win
WKAQ TV channel 2 in San Juan, PR (TM) begins broadcasting
NZ cricket all out for 26 v England at Eden Park
1st National Curling Championship held
11 days after Tibet uprising, China dissolves Tibet's government & installs Panchen Lama
Pope John appoints the first Japanese, African & Filipino cardinals
Scotch whisky factory explodes burying 20 fire fighters in Glasgow, Scotland
Military coup in Syria, President Nazim al-Kudsi flees
AFL's NY Titan's become the NY Jets
Discovery of  Epstein-Barr virus, the first human tumor virus, identified by pathologists English Anthony Epstein and Yvonne Barr published in "Lancet"
First true pirate radio station, England's Radio Caroline begins regular transmission at noon from the MV Caroline off the coast of Suffolk
Greek poet and Nobel Prize laureate Giorgos Seferis makes a famous statement on the BBC World Service opposing the junta in Greece
1,086 die when 7.4 quake destroys 254 villages in Gediz Turkey
25th Tony Awards: "Sleuth" (Best Play) & "Company" (Best Musical, Original Score, Director) win
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Rock group Raspberries breakup
NHL Washington Capitals win 1st game on road after 37 straight road loses
AIAW Women's Basketball Tournament, Delta States beat Immaculata, 69-64 at Pennsylvania State University
Morarji Desai forms a government in India
Daryl Gates becomes the 49th Chief of the Los Angeles Police Department
A partial meltdown at the Three Mile Island nuclear plant in the US results in the release of radioactive gas and iodine into the atmosphere, but no deaths
British government of Prime Minister Jim Callaghan (Labour Party) falls
Frank Lazarus & Dick Vosburgh's stage musical comedy "A Day in Hollywood/A Night in Ukraine" premieres at the Mayfair Theatre, London, England
East German Christa Rothenburger skates ladies' world record 500 m (40.18 sec); she later breaks her own record twice
France performs nuclear test
Gabi Schonbrunn skates ladies world record 3 km (4:21.70)
Yevgeni Kulikov skates world record 500m (36.91 secs)
12th Easter Seal Telethon raises $19,500,000
JN Duartes' Christian Democrats win elections in El Salvador
International Cometary Explorer measures solar wind ahead of Halley
STS 51-D vehicle moves to launch pad
Extremist Sikhs kill 13 Hindus in Ludhiana, India
John N McMahon, ends term as deputy director of CIA
Stacking of Discovery's SRBs gets underway
New Zealand wins America's Cup over Stars & Stripes, in a NY court
Bengal beat Delhi in rained-out cricket Ranji Trophy final on quotient
Ann Transon runs female world record 50k (3:35:31)
PBA National Championship Won by Eric Forkel
Conservatives win French parliamentary election
Nabisco Dinah Shore Women's Golf, Mission Hills CC: Helen Alfredsson of Sweden wins her only major title, 2 strokes ahead of 3 runners-up; first non-American to win the event as a major
Type II supernova detected in M81 (NGC 3031)
Armed Zulus demonstrate in Johannesburg, over 53 killed
BBC Radio Five Live broadcasts for first time in United Kingdom
World's largest bank - Japan's Mitsubishi Bank  and the Bank of Tokyo merge
"Seven Guitars" opens at Walter Kerr Theater NYC
"City" soap opera's final episode on ABC-TV
A Murray County, Georgia, school bus is hit by a CSX freight train (3 children die in this accident).
In a "friendly fire" incident, two US A-10 Thunderbolt IIs from the 190th Fighter Squadron attack British tanks participating in the invasion of Iraq, killing British soldier Matty Hull
Kraft Nabisco Championship Women's Golf, Mission Hills CC: Grace Park wins her only major title by 1 stroke, sinking a 6-foot birdie putt on the 72nd hole; fellow South Korean Aree Song is runner-up
The 2005 Sumatran earthquake rocks Indonesia, and at magnitude 8.7 is the second strongest earthquake since 1960.
At least 1 million union members, students and unemployed take to the streets in France in protest at the government's proposed First Employment Contract law
Sri Lankan cricket fast bowler Lasith Malinga produces unprecedented sequence of 4 wickets in 4 balls, as South Africa scrambles to a 1-wicket Super 8's ICC World Cup win in Guyana
143 rebels and 20 government troop are killed in conflict in Pibor County, Sudan
15 students are killed and 7 are injured after a mortar strikes Damascus University
Banks in Cyprus re-open after having been closed for two weeks; the government agrees a 10 billion euro bailout deal with the EU and IMF
2 cases of Ebola are reported in Liberia among people who have travelled to Guinea
Russia increases the price of gas to Ukraine by 80%
Britain introduces 1st new pound coin in 30 years with secret security feature inside to stop counterfeiting
Cyclone Debbie strikes north east Queensland coast as a category 4 storm
US confirms likely it is behind the air strike in Mosul, Iraq, that led to collapse of a building that killed more than 100 civilians
World's largest dinosaur footprint, measuring 1.7 meters, is found in Kimberley, Western Australia
At least 68 people die and scores are injured in a fire caused by a prison riot in the cells at the Carabobo state police headquarters in Valencia, Venezuela
Captain Steve Smith and David Warner are suspended by Cricket Australia for 12 months, and Cameron Bancroft (9 months) for their part in the infamous ball-tampering scandal in South Africa
European parliament bans single-use plastics, including cutlery and straws by 2021
Study of a 71 year-old British woman who has never felt pain, for the first time understood as due to a gene mutation, published in British Journal of Anaesthesia
US PresidentDonald Trumpmakes projection that 240,000 American could die from COVID-19, even with restrictions in place
Mexico revises its COVID-19 death toll up by 60% to more than 321,000 giving it the second highest COVID death toll in the world
Bus crash kills 45 Easter pilgrims from Botswana in South Africa, with one child surviving a 50 meter drop off the Mmamatlakala bridge, near Mokopane
Italian designer Alessandro Michele, previously at Gucci, is named the new creative director at French Maison de Couture Valentino
Sam Bankman-Fried, founder of FTX exchange, sentenced to 25 years in prison, after being convicted of fraud, conspiracy and money laundering

7.7 magnitude earthquake, followed by 6.4 magnitude aftershock strikes central Myanmar, causing devastating damage across Southeast Asia, leaving more than 4,400 people dead, at least 7,000 injured, and hundreds of missing persons
Roman Emperor Gaius Julius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, better known asCaligula(which means "little soldier's boots"), accepts the titles of the Principate, entitled to him by the Senate
Philip II of Spainis formally crowned King of Spain
US Senate censures PresidentAndrew Jacksonfor taking federal deposits from Bank of US
First orchestral performance ofJohannes Brahms' Serenade No. 1
"Greatest Show On Earth" formed byP. T. BarnumandJames Anthony Bailey
First performance of Hubert Parry's setting of Wiliam Blake's poem "Jerusalem", by the City of London School choir a 'Fight for Right' meeting at the Queen’s Hall, London
Tomáš Masarykelected President of Czechoslovakia
1st performance ofWalter Piston's Suite for orchestra (Boston)
Influential Nazi Propaganda film "Triumph of the Will" released showing Nuremberg rallies, commissioned byAdolf Hitlerand directed byLeni Riefenstahl
Robert Goddarduses gyroscopes to control a rocket
Philip Barry's comic play "Philadelphia Story" about a socialite's impending wedding premieres at the Shubert Theater in NYC; starKatharine Hepburnalso led the 1940 film version
Spanish Civil War ends as Madrid falls to the Nationalists headed byFrancisco Franco
Astrid Lindgrensprains ankle and begins writing "Pippi Longstocking"
2nd Tony Awards: "Mister Roberts" (Play);Henry Fonda(Actor) and Joshua Logan (Director) win
Devastating 8 for 6 spell by West Indian spin bowlerLance Gibbsgives home side innings & 30 run 3rd Test win over India in Bridgetown
Madame Tussaud's Wax Museum in London unveils figures of The Beatles; the waxworks later appear on the Sgt. Pepper album cover
UN Secretary GeneralU Thantmakes public proposals for peace in Vietnam
PopePaul VInames JGM Willebrands cardinal
Last original edition of "TheEd SullivanShow" broadcasts on CBS-TV
Elvis Presleyrecords his final Top Ten hit, a cover of "Burning Love", a song written by Dennis Linde, and first recorded by Arthur Alexander
Wilt Chamberlainplays his last pro basketball game
49th Academy Awards: "Rocky",Peter Finch&Faye Dunawaywin
Martina Navratilovabeats 16-year-old American tennis prodigyAndrea Jaeger6-3, 7-6 in the final of the WTA Tour Championships at Madison Square Garden, NYC
Viv Richardsscores century in the 1st Test at his home Antigua
Neil Simon's play "Biloxi Blues" premieres in NYC
Michael Jordanscores 69 points, 4th time he scores 60 pts in a game
US PresidentGeorge H. W. Bushposthumously awardsJesse Owensthe Congressional Gold Medal
Mike Tysonadmits paternity in Manhattan Family Court to Kimberly Scarborough's infant daughter
Italy's right-wing alliance underSilvio Berlusconiwins election
Pope Francisbecomes the first Pope to wash the feet of women in the Maundy Thursday service
US PresidentDonald Trumpsigns Energy Independence executive order undoingObamaclimate-control measures
North Korea'sKim Jong-unmeets Chinese leaderXi Jinpingin Beijing, China, his first trip outside of North Korea since coming to power in 2011
ActorWill Smithissues an apology on Instagram to Oscars hostChris Rockfor slapping him live on stage during the ceremony
Florida GovernorRon DeSantissigns so called "Don't Say Gay" bill limiting LGBTQ classroom instruction
Chris Sharmacompletes the first redpoint of Sleeping Lion in Siurana, Spain, a 9b+ (5.15c) route
US Vice PresidentKamala Harrisvisits former slave port Cape Coast Castle in Ghana as part of her tour of Ghana, Tanzania and Zambia
US Salvation Army officially organized
First seaplane takes off from water under its own power, piloted by Henri Fabre from the Étang de Berre lagoon in Martigues, France
Turkish cities Constantinople and Angora change their names to Istanbul and Ankara
The Louvre Pyramid designed byI. M. Peiis inaugurated by French President François Mitterrand in Paris
The first cases of H1N1 swine flu in the United States occur in two people in California
